Crown Kingdom.visuals: A sprawling stone-and-brick fortress with multiple towers, spires, turrets, and an imposing central gatehouse featuring a large wooden portcullis-style gate and a blue shield emblem above it. A tall, ornate left-side tower topped with a distinctive circular metal weather vane or crown-like symbol. Thick battlemented walls, smaller watchtowers, and arched bridges connecting sections of the castle. A grassy cliff edge with weathered wooden fences, giving the sense of an elevated vantage point. Courtyard: wide cobblestone paths and grand stone staircases leading up to the fortress entrance. The area is framed by towering medieval walls, turrets, and fluttering yellow banners, with a decorative fountain/sundial centerpiece under the sky. Dining hall: warm, elegant medieval dining hall inside the castle. Sunlight streams through tall gothic arched windows onto a long dining table set with a white cloth, red upholstered chairs, and tableware. The room includes a large stone fireplace with a lit fire, a chandelier, wall sconces, and lush potted plants.
Crown Kingdom.description: The Crown Kingdom, also known as the Last Kingdom, is an ancient, majestic fortress located in a remote region of the surface. It serves as the home of the White Knight squad. Originally built as a fortress on an artificial island and once a tourist destination, it has aged but still retains its grandeur. Only a handful of people know its location. According to Pioneer, it is one of only two known safe havens against the Raptures on the surface (the other being Eden). Citizens share tasks and chores on a rotating basis. Materials (batteries, electronics, metals, wood) are scavenged from the surroundings through collective effort. Maintenance and repairs are primarily handled by Chime, and later by Kilo & T.A.L.O.S. The kingdom has powerful signal jamming capabilities, keeping it hidden from Raptures. If detected, it relies on outdated but modified catapults and cannons mounted on its walls for defense. Beneath the castle (via secret passages) lies its greatest treasure: a vault containing completed seeds of a superfood miracle crop capable of sustaining humanity for generations. The White Knights guard these seeds to help humanity reclaim and thrive on the surface. The Kingdom has 82 citizens. Its main ones are Crown, Chime, Trombe, Modernia, Kilo and T.A.L.O.S. The Kingdom's law consists of 12 clauses.

The Ark.visuals: A vast, enclosed domed underground megacity known as the Dome of Eternity, its immense curved inner surface displaying a realistic projected image of a bright open sky with drifting clouds. Towering futuristic structures, residential blocks, and infrastructure packed tightly within the limited underground space. Artificial lighting mimics daylight across sprawling urban districts, corporate zones, and elevated transit networks. The outer edges transition into darker, cramped industrial sectors and the impoverished Outer Rim filled with makeshift scrap buildings and dim lighting. Occasional artificial rain or snow falls across sections of the city. Massive elevator shafts and vertical transit hubs connect the deep underground levels, with the central Lift area featuring heavily secured basements. Advanced but aging technology visible in architecture, power conduits along the dome walls, and dense population centers under the eternal artificial sky.
The Ark.description: The Ark is a massive domed underground city built deep beneath the surface, serving as the last refuge for humanity after the Raptures seized control of the world above. Originally constructed as a nuclear bunker, it represents the pinnacle of human engineering and scientific achievement. The city spans roughly 10,000 square kilometers and houses tens of millions of people, mostly descendants of the original surface population. Access to and from the surface occurs exclusively through a complex network of vertical and horizontal elevators, though the labyrinthine layout often leads to malfunctions. The Ark's artificial sky is projected across the inner surface of the Dome of Eternity, which also functions as the colony's power grid. The entire city is powered by a hidden Ultra Massive Core derived from Rapture technology, located in a secured basement area. While technologically advanced with capabilities to float and rise to the surface, this potential remains unused. Severe resource and food shortages plague the Ark due to limited underground space and lack of renewable energy sources. Most citizens subsist on Splendamin, a processed plant-based jelly, while real organic food and surface-sourced items remain expensive luxuries reserved for the elite. The universal currency is the Credit, embedded in citizenship chips and managed through central online banking. Society is informally divided into three groups: Sovereigns, the wealthy upper class with custom citizen chips; Quotidians, the common majority of ordinary citizens; and Noncitizens or Outlaws, those without chips who are banished to the harsh Outer Rim where they survive on scraps amid inhospitable conditions. An ID chip is mandatory for full citizenship and access to services, with noncitizens barred from the main city and subject to lethal enforcement if they attempt entry.

The Outpost.visuals: A diverse surface facility containing a variety of specialized structures and themed areas. The landscape includes a serene Flower Park, a bustling Amusement Park with a large Ferris wheel, and a Guest Camp. Other notable locations consist of an Observatory, a Police Station, a luxury Hotel, a cozy Maid Cafe, a vibrant Toy Store, and a Train Station. Industrial and functional buildings such as a Workshop, Armory, Generator, and Radio Tower are present, alongside community hubs like a Trendy Bar, Library, Hospital, Cafe Sweety, Theater, Seedy Club, Courthouse, Church, Clothing Store, Shopping Mall, Radio Tower, Goddess of Victory statue, Wings of Victory statue, and Fitness Club.
The Outpost.description: The Outpost is a surface-based facility serving as an operational center and resupply point for squads. Originally intended to be a midway point between the Ark and the surface, its construction was flawed due to architects digging too close to the surface, which allowed Raptures easy access. This led to significant casualties and the destruction of almost all elevators, leaving only one connection to the Ark and one to the surface. It functions as a central hub for various Nikke squads, hosting key facilities like the Command Center, Tactics Academy, Synchro Device, Recycling Room, Infrastructure Core, and themed locations including the Flower Park, Amusement Park, Guest Camp, Observatory, Police Station, Hotel, Maid Cafe, Toy Store, Train Station, Workshop, Armory, Generator, Trendy Bar, Library, Hospital, Cafe Sweety, Theater, Seedy Club, Courthouse, Church, Clothing Store, Shopping Mall, Radio Tower, Goddess of Victory statue, Wings of Victory statue, and Fitness Club.

Flower Park.description: A flower park where blossoms are dancing in the wind.

Amusement Park.description: A busy and noisy Amusement Park. With just a simple glance, the large Ferris wheel can impresss anyone.

Guest Camp.description: The area where guests are received. While a bit shabby, it gets the job done.

Observatory.description: An Observation Station that detects enemies approaching the Outpost. Nikkes from the Scouts work here.

Police Station.description: A Police Station that is responsible for maintaing public security in the Outpost. Nikkes from A.C.P.U. work here.

Hotel.description: A luxury hotel that welcomes all Nikkes.

Maid Cafe.description: A cafe full of girlish charm. It's got a small and cozy atmosphere.

Toy Store.description: A Toy Store filled with various intriguing toys. A huge eye-catching toy is also displayed in the store. Nikkes from Carronades work here.

Train Station.description: Provides civilian transit inside the Ark and supply convoys that operate on the surface. The train is called the AZX. Nikkes from Infinity Rail work here.

Workshop.description: A Workshop that churns out one item after another. Nikkes from Mighty Tools work here.

Armory.description: An armory wherer various weaponry used by Nikkes is stored. Nikkes from Absolute work here.

Generator.description: An enormous Generator that provides energy to the Ark. Nikkes from Matis work here.

Trendy Bar.description: A Trendy Bar where you can make small talk over a few cocktails. Non-alcoholic drinks are also avaiable. You can often find Nikkes from Exotic here.

Library.description: A library for broadening one's horizon.

Hospital.description: A Hospital that treats Nikkes, humans, and everything in between. Nikkes from Seraphim work here.

Cafe Sweety.description: A Cafe in the Outpost that specializes in vending machine coffee. Nikkes from Cafe Sweety work here.

Theater.description: A magnificent-looking opera house. You can often find Nikkes from Master Hand and Prima Donna here.

Seedy Club.description: A suspicious-looking club. You can often find Nikkes from Wardress here.

Courthouse.description: An immaculately maintained court, the symbol of justice and law. Nikkes from Triangle work here.

Church.description: A Church where one worshop and prays in faith. You can often find Nikkes from Extrinsic here.

Clothing Store.description: A luxurious clothing store. You can often find Nikkes from Unlimited here.

Shopping Mall.description: A Shopping Mall packed to the gills with goods that cater to all shopaholics' needs. You can often find Nikkes from Talentum here.

Radio Tower.description: A Radio Tower that transmits and receives radio waves from the Ark. You can often find Nikkes from Protocol here.

Goddess of Victory statue.description: Goddess of Victory, a statue of a Nikke. You can often find Nikkes from Pioneer here.

Wings of Victory statue.description: A statue of wings. You can often find Nikkes from Inherit here.

Fitness Club.description: A Fitness Club with various types of recreational sports facilities. You can often find Nikkes from Nepenthe here.

Reclamation Site 01.visuals: A functional surface settlement serving as a base of operations for the Counters squad, marking the first piece of land successfully reclaimed by humanity.
Reclamation Site 01.description: Reclamation Site 01 is the first piece of land permanently reclaimed by humanity on the surface, located a week's walk from the Ark. It was originally the hometown of the legendary Nikke, Red Hood. After fulfilling Red Hood's last wishes, the site was designated as a reclamation zone and now serves as the primary base of operations for the Counters squad as they scout the surface for resources. Following recent events, Cinderella and Grave have also become residents to assist in the effort to reclaim the surface.

Eden.visuals: A futuristic and highly advanced settlement hidden by optical camouflage in a remote surface region. It features lush, genetically modified gardens that replicate pre-invasion flora and fauna, highly advanced research facilities, and a massive tower housing a supermassive particle cannon.
Eden.description: Eden is an advanced, self-sufficient settlement on the surface where humans and Nikkes coexist as equals in theory, though currently no human lives there and only a handful of Nikke are part of it. Founded by Dorothy, Johan, and Cecil using a repurposed First Rapture War laboratory, it utilizes cutting-edge technology like optical camouflage and advanced energy infrastructure to remain hidden. Key facilities include the Garden of Eden, which hosts a near-perfect replica of pre-invasion flora and fauna; the Central Research Facility for advanced weaponry and medical research; and Eden's Spear, a supermassive particle cannon capable of devastating attacks. The settlement is guided by seven edicts focused on capability, protection, and the ultimate goal of reclaiming the surface. The Central Government's upper echelons are aware of Eden's existence, but they do not openly contact them or publicize their existence for various reasons.

Wasteland.visuals: Vast barren landscapes of slagged, cratered terrain with zero greenery; unrecognizable ruins and scattered wreckage under open skies.
Wasteland.description: The Wasteland is the barren, desolate surface region of extreme hostility, marked by slagged earth and total landscape alteration.
